title: cestrum capsulare
intro: Cestrum capsulare, also known as the Cape Chestnut, is a flowering plant native to South Africa and parts of the Indian Ocean. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Cestrum capsulare include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments.
